content: The rise of antibiotic resistance is a growing concern that threatens to undermine our ability to treat common infections. The main driver of antibiotic resistance is the overuse and misuse of antibiotics in human medicine and agriculture. For instance, it's estimated that up to 50% of antibiotic prescriptions in the US are unnecessary or inappropriate. This misuse can stem from doctors prescribing antibiotics for viral infections or from patients demanding antibiotics for minor ailments. In agriculture, antibiotics are often used as growth promoters in livestock, leading to the development of resistant bacteria in farm environments. These resistant bacteria can then be transferred to humans through the food chain or environmental contamination. To combat this, we must adopt a multi-faceted approach, including improved antibiotic stewardship and responsible use in agriculture. Individuals can contribute by using antibiotics only when prescribed and supporting antibiotic-free farming practices. By working together, we can reduce the spread of antibiotic resistance and ensure these life-saving medicines remain effective. The future of antibiotic effectiveness is in our hands, and the time to act is now.
